Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of The First Amended Complaint To Specially Appearing Defendant Alex Espinoza
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Maryam Maleki, Esq. of Manning & Kass, Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP, Tel. 415.400.0859, for defendant and moving party;
Stephen F. Yurcich, Esq. of Kaplan Law Firm, Tel. 619.232.1991 ext. 129, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALEX ESPINOZA'S Motion To Quash Service Of The First Amended Complaint is granted. The motion is moot as to the July service as plaintiff attempted to serve defendant in September and does not contend that the July service effectuated jurisdiction. The motion is granted as to the September service. "When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process 'the burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service.'" (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413.) Here, the declaration from Mr. Espinoza demonstrates that he does not reside at 750 Ada Street, Apt. 15, Chula Vista, CA 91911. Therefore, the substitute service at that address was improper. (See CCP 415.20(b).) The court further notes that the declaration of Mr. DePinerez is based on the hearsay statement of Ms. Ruiz regarding where Mr. Espinoza resides. Mr. Espinoza's motion for terminating or monetary sanctions is denied as totally baseless.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.
